{"metadata":{"kernelspec":{"language":"python","display_name":"Python 3","name":"python3"},"language_info":{"name":"python","version":"3.12.13","mimetype":"text/x-python","codemirror_mode":{"name":"ipython","version":3},"pygments_lexer":"ipython3","nbconvert_exporter":"python","file_extension":".py"},"kaggle":{"accelerator":"none","dataSources":[],"dockerImageVersionId":28755,"isInternetEnabled":false,"language":"python","sourceType":"notebook","isGpuEnabled":false}},"nbformat_minor":4,"nbformat":4,"cells":[{"cell_type":"markdown","source":"# Molecular Dynamics Simulation of Lennard-Jones Fluid\n\nA 2D molecular dynamics simulation of argon-like particles interacting via \nthe Lennard-Jones potential, implemented in the microcanonical (NVE) ensemble.\n\n**System:** N=25 particles in a square box with periodic boundary conditions  \n**Integrator:** Störmer-Verlet  \n**Units:** Lennard-Jones reduced units (ε=1, σ=1, m=1)","metadata":{}},{"cell_type":"code","source":"import numpy as np","metadata":{"trusted":true,"execution":{"iopub.status.busy":"2026-06-27T12:07:37.836134Z","iopub.execute_input":"2026-06-27T12:07:37.836604Z","iopub.status.idle":"2026-06-27T12:07:37.842445Z","shell.execute_reply.started":"2026-06-27T12:07:37.836572Z","shell.execute_reply":"2026-06-27T12:07:37.841511Z"}},"outputs":[],"execution_count":71},{"cell_type":"code","source":"# Simulation parameters (Lennard-Jones reduced units)\nN  = 25          # number of particles\nrho = 0.8        # number density (liquid-like LJ state)\nL  = np.sqrt(N / rho)  # box side length\nsq = int(np.sqrt(N))   # particles per row (square lattice)\na  = L / sq            # lattice spacing\n\nm       = 1.0    # particle mass\nsigma   = 1.0    # LJ length scale\nepsilon = 1.0    # LJ energy scale\ndt      = 0.005  # time step\nv0      = 1.0    # initial speed\nTime    = 5000   # number of time steps\nseed    = 32543  # random seed for reproducibility","metadata":{"trusted":true,"execution":{"iopub.status.busy":"2026-06-27T12:07:37.905150Z","iopub.execute_input":"2026-06-27T12:07:37.905734Z","iopub.status.idle":"2026-06-27T12:07:37.911745Z","shell.execute_reply.started":"2026-06-27T12:07:37.905701Z","shell.execute_reply":"2026-06-27T12:07:37.910955Z"}},"outputs":[],"execution_count":72},{"cell_type":"code","source":"# Arrays  (rows = particles, cols = time steps)\nx    = np.zeros((N, Time))   # wrapped x-coordinate\ny    = np.zeros((N, Time))   # wrapped y-coordinate\nx_uw = np.zeros((N, Time))   # unwrapped x-coordinate (for MSD)\ny_uw = np.zeros((N, Time))   # unwrapped y-coordinate (for MSD)\nvx   = np.zeros((N, Time))   # x-velocity\nvy   = np.zeros((N, Time))   # y-velocity\nax   = np.zeros((N, Time))   # x-acceleration\nay   = np.zeros((N, Time))   # y-acceleration\nv    = np.zeros((N, Time))   # speed\nr    = np.zeros((N, N, Time))# pairwise distances\nKinetic   = np.zeros(Time)\nPotential = np.zeros(Time)\nEnergy    = np.zeros(Time)\n\n\ndef lj_force(rij):\n    \"\"\"Magnitude of LJ force divided by rij (for projection).\"\"\"\n    return 24.0 * epsilon * (2.0 * (sigma / rij)**12 - (sigma / rij)**6) / rij\n\n\ndef lj_potential(rij):\n    \"\"\"LJ pair potential.\"\"\"\n    return 4.0 * epsilon * ((sigma / rij)**12 - (sigma / rij)**6)\n\n\ndef pbc_wrap(x, y):\n    \"\"\"Apply periodic boundary conditions — wrap coordinates into [0, L).\"\"\"\n    if x > L: x -= L\n    if x < 0: x += L\n    if y > L: y -= L\n    if y < 0: y += L\n    return x, y\n\n\ndef pbc_diff(dx, dy):\n    \"\"\"Minimum image convention for displacement vectors.\"\"\"\n    if dx >  L / 2.0: dx -= L\n    if dx < -L / 2.0: dx += L\n    if dy >  L / 2.0: dy -= L\n    if dy < -L / 2.0: dy += L\n    return dx, dy","metadata":{"trusted":true,"execution":{"iopub.status.busy":"2026-06-27T12:07:37.970039Z","iopub.execute_input":"2026-06-27T12:07:37.970705Z","iopub.status.idle":"2026-06-27T12:07:37.993094Z","shell.execute_reply.started":"2026-06-27T12:07:37.970673Z","shell.execute_reply":"2026-06-27T12:07:37.992051Z"}},"outputs":[],"execution_count":73},{"cell_type":"markdown","source":"## 2. Subroutines\n\n- **`initialize`**: places particles on a square lattice with random velocity directions\n- **`compute_acceleration`**: computes LJ forces on each particle using the minimum image convention\n- **`compute_energy`**: computes kinetic, potential, and total energy at each time step","metadata":{}},{"cell_type":"code","source":"def initialize(sq, a, N, L, v0, x, y, x_uw, y_uw, vx, vy, v):\n    \"\"\"Place particles on a square lattice with random velocities.\"\"\"\n    np.random.seed(seed)\n\n    for i in range(sq):\n        for j in range(sq):\n            k = sq * i + j\n            x[k, 0]    = (i + 0.5) * a\n            y[k, 0]    = (j + 0.5) * a\n            x_uw[k, 0] = x[k, 0]\n            y_uw[k, 0] = y[k, 0]\n\n    for i in range(N):\n        theta   = (np.random.rand() - 0.5) * 2.0 * np.pi\n        vx[i, 0] = v0 * np.cos(theta)\n        vy[i, 0] = v0 * np.sin(theta)\n        v[i, 0]  = v0\n\n    for i in range(N):\n        x[i, 0], y[i, 0] = pbc_wrap(x[i, 0], y[i, 0])\n\n    return x, y, x_uw, y_uw, vx, vy, v\n\n\ndef compute_acceleration(i, n, x, y, ax, ay, r):\n    \"\"\"Compute LJ acceleration on particle i at time step n.\"\"\"\n    ax[i, n] = 0.0\n    ay[i, n] = 0.0\n    for j in range(N):\n        if j == i:\n            r[i, j, n] = 0.0\n            continue\n        dxij, dyij = pbc_diff(x[i, n] - x[j, n], y[i, n] - y[j, n])\n        rij = np.sqrt(dxij**2 + dyij**2)\n        r[i, j, n] = rij\n        ff = lj_force(rij)\n        ax[i, n] += ff * dxij / (m * rij)\n        ay[i, n] += ff * dyij / (m * rij)\n    return ax, ay, r\n\n\ndef compute_energy(v, r, Kinetic, Potential, Energy):\n    \"\"\"Compute kinetic, potential, and total energy at each time step.\"\"\"\n    for n in range(Time):\n        Kinetic[n]   = 0.5 * m * np.sum(v[:, n]**2)\n        Potential[n] = sum(lj_potential(r[i, j, n])\n                           for i in range(N)\n                           for j in range(i+1, N)\n                           if r[i, j, n] > 0.0)\n        Energy[n] = Kinetic[n] + Potential[n]\n    return Kinetic, Potential, Energy\n\n","metadata":{"trusted":true,"execution":{"iopub.status.busy":"2026-06-27T12:07:38.039564Z","iopub.execute_input":"2026-06-27T12:07:38.040058Z","iopub.status.idle":"2026-06-27T12:07:38.054287Z","shell.execute_reply.started":"2026-06-27T12:07:38.040023Z","shell.execute_reply":"2026-06-27T12:07:38.053342Z"}},"outputs":[],"execution_count":null},{"cell_type":"markdown","source":"## 3. Time Integration\n\nThe equations of motion are integrated using the Störmer-Verlet algorithm.  \nUnwrapped coordinates (`x_uw`, `y_uw`) are tracked alongside wrapped ones \nto enable correct MSD calculation across periodic boundaries.","metadata":{}},{"cell_type":"code","source":"# ── Initialization ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\nx, y, x_uw, y_uw, vx, vy, v = initialize(sq, a, N, L, v0, x, y, x_uw, y_uw, vx, vy, v)\n\n# ── Initial accelerations (required for first Verlet step) ──────────────────\nfor i in range(N):\n    ax, ay, r = compute_acceleration(i, 0, x, y, ax, ay, r)\n\n# ── First step: Velocity Verlet (n=0 → n=1) ─────────────────────────────────\nfor i in range(N):\n    x_new = x[i, 0] + vx[i, 0] * dt + 0.5 * ax[i, 0] * dt**2\n    y_new = y[i, 0] + vy[i, 0] * dt + 0.5 * ay[i, 0] * dt**2\n\n    dx_step, dy_step = pbc_diff(x_new - x[i, 0], y_new - y[i, 0])\n    x_uw[i, 1] = x_uw[i, 0] + dx_step\n    y_uw[i, 1] = y_uw[i, 0] + dy_step\n\n    x[i, 1], y[i, 1] = pbc_wrap(x_new, y_new)\n\n# ── Main Verlet loop ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\nfor n in range(1, Time - 1):\n    for i in range(N):\n        ax, ay, r = compute_acceleration(i, n, x, y, ax, ay, r)\n\n        x_new = 2*x[i, n] - x[i, n-1] + ax[i, n] * dt**2\n        y_new = 2*y[i, n] - y[i, n-1] + ay[i, n] * dt**2\n\n        dx_step, dy_step = pbc_diff(x_new - x[i, n], y_new - y[i, n])\n        x_uw[i, n+1] = x_uw[i, n] + dx_step\n        y_uw[i, n+1] = y_uw[i, n] + dy_step\n\n        x[i, n+1], y[i, n+1] = pbc_wrap(x_new, y_new)\n\n        dx, dy = pbc_diff(x[i, n+1] - x[i, n-1], y[i, n+1] - y[i, n-1])\n        vx[i, n] = 0.5 * dx / dt\n        vy[i, n] = 0.5 * dy / dt\n        v[i, n]  = np.sqrt(vx[i, n]**2 + vy[i, n]**2)","metadata":{"trusted":true,"execution":{"iopub.status.busy":"2026-06-27T12:07:38.125699Z","iopub.execute_input":"2026-06-27T12:07:38.126521Z","iopub.status.idle":"2026-06-27T12:07:51.935534Z","shell.execute_reply.started":"2026-06-27T12:07:38.126486Z","shell.execute_reply":"2026-06-27T12:07:51.934758Z"}},"outputs":[],"execution_count":75},{"cell_type":"code","source":"# ── Energy calculation ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────\nKinetic, Potential, Energy = compute_energy(v, r, Kinetic, Potential, Energy)","metadata":{"trusted":true,"execution":{"iopub.status.busy":"2026-06-27T12:07:51.937252Z","iopub.execute_input":"2026-06-27T12:07:51.937603Z","iopub.status.idle":"2026-06-27T12:07:53.828235Z","shell.execute_reply.started":"2026-06-27T12:07:51.937567Z","shell.execute_reply":"2026-06-27T12:07:53.827444Z"}},"outputs":[],"execution_count":76},{"cell_type":"markdown","source":"## 4. Energy Conservation\n\nIn the NVE ensemble, total energy must be conserved.  \nFluctuations in kinetic and potential energy are expected and reflect \nthe natural exchange between degrees of freedom.","metadata":{}},{"cell_type":"code","source":"# ── Energy Conservation ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────\nimport matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n\nplt.figure(figsize=(8, 4))\nplt.plot(Energy,    label='Total Energy')\nplt.plot(Kinetic,   label='Kinetic Energy')\nplt.plot(Potential, label='Potential Energy')\nplt.xlabel('Time step')\nplt.ylabel('Energy (LJ units)')\nplt.title('Energy Conservation')\nplt.legend()\nplt.tight_layout()\nplt.savefig('energy.png')\nplt.show()","metadata":{"trusted":true,"execution":{"iopub.status.busy":"2026-06-27T12:07:53.829285Z","iopub.execute_input":"2026-06-27T12:07:53.829952Z","iopub.status.idle":"2026-06-27T12:07:54.268413Z","shell.execute_reply.started":"2026-06-27T12:07:53.829888Z","shell.execute_reply":"2026-06-27T12:07:54.267656Z"}},"outputs":[{"output_type":"display_data","data":{"text/plain":"<Figure size 800x400 with 1 Axes>","image/png":"\n"},"metadata":{}}],"execution_count":77},{"cell_type":"markdown","source":"## 5. Radial Distribution Function\n\ng(r) describes the probability of finding a particle at distance r \nfrom a reference particle, relative to an ideal gas.  \nA liquid-like state shows a pronounced first peak near r≈σ, \nfollowed by damped oscillations converging to g(r)→1.","metadata":{}},{"cell_type":"code","source":"# ── Radial Distribution Function ─────────────────────────────────────────────\ndef compute_rdf(r, N, Time, L, dr=0.1, equilib=1000):\n    \"\"\"Compute g(r) averaged over frames after equilibration.\"\"\"\n    r_max  = L / 2.0\n    bins   = np.arange(0, r_max, dr)\n    rdf    = np.zeros(len(bins) - 1)\n    rho    = N / L**2\n    count  = 0\n\n    for n in range(equilib, Time):\n        for i in range(N):\n            for j in range(i+1, N):\n                if 0 < r[i, j, n] < r_max:\n                    idx = int(r[i, j, n] / dr)\n                    if idx < len(rdf):\n                        rdf[idx] += 2\n        count += 1\n\n    r_centers = bins[:-1] + dr / 2\n    for k in range(len(rdf)):\n        shell_area = 2 * np.pi * r_centers[k] * dr\n        rdf[k] /= (count * N * rho * shell_area)\n\n    return r_centers, rdf\n\nr_centers, rdf = compute_rdf(r, N, Time, L)\n\nplt.figure(figsize=(7, 4))\nplt.plot(r_centers, rdf)\nplt.axhline(1.0, color='gray', linestyle='--', label='ideal gas limit')\nplt.xlabel('r / σ')\nplt.ylabel('g(r)')\nplt.title('Radial Distribution Function')\nplt.legend()\nplt.tight_layout()\nplt.savefig('rdf.png')\nplt.show()","metadata":{"trusted":true,"execution":{"iopub.status.busy":"2026-06-27T12:07:54.270347Z","iopub.execute_input":"2026-06-27T12:07:54.270747Z","iopub.status.idle":"2026-06-27T12:07:55.538885Z","shell.execute_reply.started":"2026-06-27T12:07:54.270718Z","shell.execute_reply":"2026-06-27T12:07:55.537829Z"}},"outputs":[{"output_type":"display_data","data":{"text/plain":"<Figure size 700x400 with 1 Axes>","image/png":"\n"},"metadata":{}}],"execution_count":78},{"cell_type":"markdown","source":"## 7. Maxwell-Boltzmann Velocity Distribution\n\nAt thermal equilibrium, each velocity component follows a Gaussian distribution:\n\n$$P(v_x) = \\frac{1}{\\sqrt{2\\pi k_BT/m}} \\exp\\left(-\\frac{mv_x^2}{2k_BT}\\right)$$\n\nThe speed distribution follows a Rayleigh distribution (2D Maxwell-Boltzmann).  \nData is taken after equilibration (step > 1000) to ensure the system \nhas reached a stationary state — consistent with the ergodic hypothesis.","metadata":{}},{"cell_type":"markdown","source":"## 6. Mean Square Displacement and Diffusion\n\nIn the liquid phase, MSD grows linearly with time (diffusive regime):\n\n$$\\langle \\Delta r^2(\\tau) \\rangle = 4D\\tau \\quad \\text{(2D)}$$\n\nThe diffusion coefficient D is extracted from a linear fit \nin the interval [T/4, T/2] to avoid ballistic and noise-dominated regimes.  \nUnwrapped coordinates are essential here — wrapped coordinates \nproduce artificial saturation due to periodic boundary jumps.","metadata":{}},{"cell_type":"code","source":"# ── Mean Square Displacement & Diffusion Coefficient ─────────────────────────\nMSD   = np.zeros(Time)\ntimes = np.arange(Time) * dt\n\nfor tau in range(1, Time):\n    dx = x_uw[:, tau:] - x_uw[:, :Time - tau]\n    dy = y_uw[:, tau:] - y_uw[:, :Time - tau]\n    MSD[tau] = np.mean(dx**2 + dy**2)\n\n# Linear fit in the diffusive regime\nt_start = Time // 4\nt_end   = Time // 2\nslope   = np.polyfit(times[t_start:t_end], MSD[t_start:t_end], 1)[0]\nD       = slope / (2 * 2)   # Einstein relation: MSD = 2d·D·t, d=2\n\nprint(f\"Diffusion Coefficient D = {D:.6f} (LJ units)\")\n\nplt.figure(figsize=(8, 4))\nplt.plot(times, MSD, label='MSD')\nplt.xlabel('Time (LJ units)')\nplt.ylabel('MSD (σ²)')\nplt.title('Mean Square Displacement')\nplt.legend()\nplt.grid(True)\nplt.tight_layout()\nplt.savefig('msd.png')\nplt.show()","metadata":{"trusted":true,"execution":{"iopub.status.busy":"2026-06-27T12:07:55.540115Z","iopub.execute_input":"2026-06-27T12:07:55.540452Z","iopub.status.idle":"2026-06-27T12:07:57.526063Z","shell.execute_reply.started":"2026-06-27T12:07:55.540413Z","shell.execute_reply":"2026-06-27T12:07:57.525069Z"}},"outputs":[{"name":"stdout","text":"Diffusion Coefficient D = 0.063492 (LJ units)\n","output_type":"stream"},{"output_type":"display_data","data":{"text/plain":"<Figure size 800x400 with 1 Axes>","image/png":"\n"},"metadata":{}}],"execution_count":79},{"cell_type":"code","source":"# ── Maxwell-Boltzmann Velocity Distribution ───────────────────────────────────\nfrom scipy.stats import norm\n\nequilib = 1000\nvx_eq   = vx[:, equilib:].flatten()\nv_eq    = v[:, equilib:].flatten()\n\nfig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 4))\n\n# vx: Gaussian\nax = axes[0]\nax.hist(vx_eq, bins=60, density=True, alpha=0.6, label='vx histogram')\nmu, std = norm.fit(vx_eq)\nx_range = np.linspace(vx_eq.min(), vx_eq.max(), 200)\nax.plot(x_range, norm.pdf(x_range, mu, std), 'r-', lw=2,\n        label=f'Gaussian fit\\nμ={mu:.3f}, σ={std:.3f}')\nax.set_xlabel('vx (LJ units)')\nax.set_ylabel('Probability density')\nax.set_title('Velocity Distribution (vx)')\nax.legend()\n\n# Speed: Rayleigh\nax = axes[1]\nax.hist(v_eq, bins=60, density=True, alpha=0.6, label='speed histogram')\nv_range        = np.linspace(0, v_eq.max(), 200)\nrayleigh_theory = (v_range / std**2) * np.exp(-v_range**2 / (2 * std**2))\nax.plot(v_range, rayleigh_theory, 'r-', lw=2,\n        label=f'Rayleigh theory\\nσ={std:.3f}')\nax.set_xlabel('|v| (LJ units)')\nax.set_ylabel('Probability density')\nax.set_title('Speed Distribution')\nax.legend()\n\nplt.tight_layout()\nplt.savefig('mb_distribution.png')\nplt.show()\n\nT_measured = std**2\nprint(f\"Temperature T = {T_measured:.4f} (LJ units)\")\nprint(f\"Density   rho = {N / L**2:.4f} (LJ units)\")","metadata":{"trusted":true,"execution":{"iopub.status.busy":"2026-06-27T12:07:57.527408Z","iopub.execute_input":"2026-06-27T12:07:57.527764Z","iopub.status.idle":"2026-06-27T12:07:58.312637Z","shell.execute_reply.started":"2026-06-27T12:07:57.527736Z","shell.execute_reply":"2026-06-27T12:07:58.311634Z"}},"outputs":[{"output_type":"display_data","data":{"text/plain":"<Figure size 1200x400 with 2 Axes>","image/png":"\n"},"metadata":{}},{"name":"stdout","text":"Temperature T = 0.3808 (LJ units)\nDensity   rho = 0.8000 (LJ units)\n","output_type":"stream"}],"execution_count":80}]}
